title: Few-Shot Examples for Coding
concept: Providing representative code templates and API usage details to shape syntax outputs.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of using markdown fences in coding?

options.text: To isolate code blocks in responses
To confuse the reader and make it harder to read
To add colors to code snippets

exercises.explain: Using markdown fences helps us clearly isolate code blocks, making them easier to read and understand.

cards.title: What are Few-Shot Examples?
cards.body: Few-shot examples are small sets of code provided to help guide an AI in generating specific code outputs.

cards.title: Definition of Few-Shot Examples
cards.body: Few-shot examples in coding are brief templates or instances that show how to use certain APIs or syntax.

cards.title: Few-Shot Examples are like Key Guides
cards.body: Imagine a map that shows you only a few landmarks. Those landmarks help you understand how to explore the entire area. Similarly, few-shot examples give AI important clues to generate the right code.

cards.title: Example of Few-Shot Code
cards.body: If we provide a few examples of how to use a function, like ‘print()’ in Python, it helps the AI understand how to format print statements correctly.

cards.title: Myth: Examples Limit Creativity
cards.body: Some think that providing examples restricts AI creativity. In truth, they provide direction, allowing for more precise coding outputs.

cards.title: Visualize Few-Shot Examples
cards.body: Think of few-shot examples as a recipe with key ingredients for coding. They lay the foundation for building the final dish. What ingredients do you need for a successful code output?

exercises.prompt: Why are few-shot examples helpful in coding?

options.text: They make the code longer and harder to read
They provide direction for generating specific code outputs
They confuse the AI and give random results
They are unnecessary and can be ignored

exercises.explain: Few-shot examples give clear guidelines for the AI, helping it generate more accurate and useful code.

exercises.prompt: Few-shot examples can help in understanding API usages better.
exercises.explain: True! Few-shot examples provide clarity on how to use APIs effectively.

exercises.prompt: Few-shot examples are important because they provide ______ for the AI to generate code.
exercises.sentence: Few-shot examples are important because they provide guidance for the AI to generate code.
exercises.explain: The correct word is 'guidance' because it shows that examples help steer the AI's coding.

exercises.prompt: Which statement is an AI-generated example about few-shot coding?

options.text: All code should be written without examples to allow for free thinking.
Few-shot examples help shape the AI's syntax outputs by showing clear patterns.
More examples make the output worse because it's hard to follow.

exercises.explain: The correct answer highlights that few-shot examples help establish clear patterns for the AI's responses.

exercises.prompt: What do few-shot examples specifically provide to help AI generate code effectively?

options.text: Confusing instructions that lead to errors

exercises.explain: Few-shot examples provide clear syntax outputs and coding patterns, which help the AI understand what to produce.
